stack: rust / axum 0.8 / tokio / redis 1 / Ory (Hydra/Kratos) reference

candidates.name: metrics + metrics-exporter-prometheus
candidates.evidence: Tier 3 cargo registry: metrics 0.24.6, metrics-exporter-prometheus 0.18.3, actively maintained. Facade decouples instrumentation call-sites from exporter; PrometheusBuilder::install_recorder() + handle.render() served on a /metrics axum route (admin port). No async-runtime coupling. Right granularity for delegate success/deny/latency counters.
rejected_alternatives: opentelemetry 0.32 — heavier, frequent breaking churn, OTLP overkill for control-plane counters
axum-prometheus 0.10 — auto HTTP-latency middleware, wrong granularity for delegate-semantic counters
prometheus 0.14 (tikv) — lower-level registry, more boilerplate than the metrics facade

build_required.rationale: RedisRateLimiter::incr_request (Lua-atomic fixed-window) already exists but is #[allow(dead_code)] and /oauth/* still uses the in-process governor. BUILD a thin Redis-backed axum layer on /oauth/* keyed by client_id (peer-IP fallback for the pre-auth token surface). tower_governor KeyExtractor is in-process only and cannot make cross-replica accurate — do NOT adopt a second rate-limit lib.
build_required.open_question: Redis-outage posture: config toggle oauth.rate_limit.on_backend_unavailable = deny | degrade; default deny for /oauth/introspect (oracle), degrade+warn for /oauth/token (availability).

build_required.rationale: No new dep. (a) https-only Hydra URL validation at startup (reject http:// unless explicit off-by-default allow_insecure_upstream); (b) body-size cap replacing unbounded resp.json::<Value>() in token_exchange.rs; (c) oauth_exposure_posture() mirroring the existing admin_auth_posture() RefuseStart pattern — refuse start when /oauth/* binds non-loopback without both introspect_auth AND rate-limiting.

DECIDED: do NOT re-stamp Hydra-minted delegated tokens with flint_kind=agent — rewriting another authority's token violates the federate-never-an-IdP constraint. Document that delegate-mode tokens carry Hydra's claims (not gateway agent-budget classification) and meter delegate issuance so the bypass volume is visible. Any future budget parity belongs in a Hydra-side claim mapper.
build_required.reuses: cand-001 metrics (to meter the documented gap)

build_required.gap: G4 E2E vs real Ory stack
build_required.rationale: ADOPT existing harness — extend docker-compose.smoke.yml with Ory Hydra/Kratos + add Playwright specs for authenticated /oauth/token + /oauth/introspect + Hydra-delegate happy-path and fail-closed denials (unauth, over-rate, Hydra error/redirect). No new test framework.
